seo.title: E-Commerce Agentic RAG AI Agent Checklist
seo.description: Production readiness checklist for agentic rag AI agents in e-commerce. Covers FTC advertising guidelines, CCPA/GDPR, PCI DSS for payments.

content.intro: Deploying an Agentic RAG system in e-commerce introduces compounding risks: autonomous query planning across product catalogs, pricing databases, and policy documents means a single retrieval error can cascade into FTC violations, PCI DSS exposure, or mass customer misinformation. Unlike static chatbots, multi-step retrieval agents synthesize from heterogeneous sources in real time, making source faithfulness and cross-source consistency non-negotiable. This checklist operationalizes the controls needed to deploy safely under FTC advertising guidelines, CCPA/GDPR, PCI DSS, and ADA accessibility requirements.

sections.heading: Source Integrity & Retrieval Governance
sections.description: Controls ensuring the agent only retrieves from verified, current data sources and that query planning does not introduce hallucinated or stale product, pricing, or policy information.

items.label: Maintain a Verified Source Registry
items.description: Enumerate every data source the agent is permitted to query (product catalog, pricing engine, returns policy CMS, inventory API). Block retrieval from any source not on the approved registry to prevent the agent from pulling unvetted third-party content into customer-facing responses.

items.label: Enforce Real-Time Pricing Sync
items.description: Connect the retrieval layer directly to the live pricing engine rather than a cached index. Implement a maximum cache TTL of 60 seconds for pricing data and trigger an automatic staleness flag if the pricing source timestamp exceeds this threshold before the agent synthesizes a response.

items.label: Validate Query Plan Against Allowed Intents
items.description: Before execution, parse the agent's generated query plan and validate each sub-query against an allowlist of permitted retrieval intents (e.g., product lookup, price check, policy retrieval). Reject or escalate plans that include payment credential lookups, PII-heavy queries, or out-of-scope data sources.

items.label: Implement Cross-Source Consistency Checks
items.description: After multi-source retrieval, run an automated consistency check that compares product claims, prices, and policy terms across all retrieved chunks. Flag responses where the same attribute (e.g., price, return window) differs by more than a defined tolerance across sources before synthesis.

items.label: Log Full Retrieval Provenance Per Response
items.description: For every agent response, persist a structured log containing: source identifiers, document version/timestamp, chunk IDs, relevance scores, and the final query plan. Retain logs for a minimum of 24 months to support FTC inquiry responses and dispute resolution.

items.label: Restrict Agent Access to Cardholder Data Environments
items.description: Enforce network segmentation so the retrieval agent cannot query systems within the PCI DSS cardholder data environment (CDE). Payment-related queries must be routed through a dedicated, audited payment microservice, not synthesized by the RAG agent.

items.label: Version-Control Policy Documents in the Knowledge Base
items.description: Store return/refund policies, warranty terms, and promotional T&Cs in a version-controlled document store. When a policy is updated, immediately re-index the new version and archive the old one with an expiry flag so the agent cannot retrieve superseded policy text.

sections.heading: Hallucination Detection & Output Verification
sections.description: Mechanisms to detect and suppress fabricated product claims, invented pricing, or misrepresented policies before they reach customers, in compliance with FTC substantiation requirements.

items.label: Deploy Grounded Attribution Scoring on Every Response
items.description: After synthesis, run an automated grounding check that maps each factual claim in the output back to a specific retrieved chunk. Assign a grounding score; suppress or escalate any response where a claim cannot be attributed to a retrieved source with confidence above a defined threshold (e.g., 0.85).

items.label: Implement Product Claim Substantiation Gate
items.description: For any response containing superlative or comparative product claims (e.g., 'best,' 'fastest,' 'lowest price'), require the agent to cite a specific, retrievable source document that substantiates the claim. Reject claims that cannot be grounded in retrieved evidence, per FTC substantiation requirements.

items.label: Run Numeric Consistency Validation on Prices and Quantities
items.description: Apply a post-synthesis validator that extracts all numeric values (prices, discounts, quantities, dimensions) from the agent's response and cross-references them against the retrieved source data. Flag any numeric discrepancy greater than 0% for prices and 5% for non-price attributes.

items.label: Surface Inline Citations to End Users
items.description: Require the agent to include inline source citations (e.g., 'per our Returns Policy, updated [date]') for all policy and pricing claims in customer-facing responses. This supports FTC transparency expectations and allows customers to independently verify information.

items.label: Establish a Human-in-the-Loop Escalation Threshold
items.description: Define confidence thresholds below which the agent must escalate to a human agent rather than synthesize a response autonomously. Apply lower thresholds for high-stakes queries (pricing disputes, refund eligibility, promotional terms) than for general product information.

items.label: Red-Team the Agent Quarterly for Prompt Injection and Hallucination
items.description: Conduct structured adversarial testing every quarter using a test suite of prompts designed to elicit hallucinated prices, fabricated return policies, and invented product specifications. Document findings and remediate within 30 days.

items.label: Monitor Production Hallucination Rate as a KPI
items.description: Instrument the agent to track the percentage of responses flagged by grounding checks in production. Set an alert threshold (e.g., >2% of responses flagged in a 1-hour window) that triggers an automatic degraded-mode fallback to static FAQ responses.

sections.heading: Privacy, Data Minimization & Regulatory Compliance
sections.description: Controls ensuring the agentic retrieval pipeline handles customer PII in compliance with CCPA and GDPR, and that advertising and promotional content meets FTC guidelines.

items.label: Enforce PII Stripping Before Retrieval Query Construction
items.description: Before the agent constructs retrieval queries, apply a PII detection and redaction layer that removes or pseudonymizes customer identifiers (email, name, address, order ID) from query strings. Queries to the knowledge base must be anonymized unless the retrieval source is explicitly scoped to authenticated user data.

items.label: Implement Data Subject Request (DSR) Propagation to RAG Index
items.description: When a CCPA deletion or GDPR erasure request is fulfilled, trigger an automated pipeline that removes the subject's data from all RAG index shards, vector stores, and retrieval caches within 72 hours. Maintain an audit log of DSR propagation completion.

items.label: Disclose AI-Generated Responses to Customers
items.description: Display a clear, persistent disclosure (e.g., 'This response was generated by an AI assistant') on all agent-synthesized outputs, per FTC guidance on AI transparency. The disclosure must be visible without scrolling and meet WCAG 2.1 AA contrast requirements.

items.label: Restrict Personalized Retrieval to Consented Data Scopes
items.description: If the agent retrieves from personalized data sources (purchase history, browsing behavior), verify that the customer has provided explicit consent for AI-driven personalization under CCPA/GDPR. Block personalized retrieval for customers who have opted out of data processing.

items.label: Audit Promotional Content Retrieval for FTC Compliance
items.description: When the agent retrieves and surfaces promotional claims, discounts, or limited-time offers, validate that the retrieved content includes required FTC disclosures (e.g., 'while supplies last,' asterisk terms). Suppress promotional content that lacks required disclosures.

items.label: Conduct Annual DPIA for Agentic Retrieval Pipeline
items.description: Perform a Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) annually covering the full retrieval pipeline, including all data sources, query logs, and synthesis outputs. Document lawful basis for processing under GDPR Article 6 and update the assessment when new data sources are added.

items.label: Ensure ADA/WCAG 2.1 AA Compliance for Agent UI
items.description: Validate that the customer-facing interface delivering agent responses meets WCAG 2.1 AA standards: keyboard navigability, screen reader compatibility, sufficient color contrast for citations and disclosures, and text alternatives for any non-text content in responses.

sections.heading: Operational Monitoring, Incident Response & Governance
sections.description: Ongoing controls to detect failures in production, respond to incidents involving incorrect pricing or policy misrepresentation, and maintain governance over the agent's autonomous behavior.

items.label: Implement Real-Time Pricing Error Alerting
items.description: Deploy a monitoring rule that compares agent-surfaced prices against the live pricing engine in real time. If the agent outputs a price that deviates from the live price by more than $0.01 or 0.1%, trigger an immediate alert to the e-commerce ops team and suppress the agent's pricing responses until the discrepancy is resolved.

items.label: Define and Test an Agent Kill Switch
items.description: Implement a documented, tested kill switch that can disable the agentic RAG system within 60 seconds and fall back to static product pages and human support. Test the kill switch monthly and include it in the incident response runbook.

items.label: Establish a Refund Dispute Attribution Process
items.description: Create a documented process for attributing refund disputes to agent-generated misinformation. When a customer disputes a purchase citing agent-provided information, retrieve the full response log and provenance chain within 4 hours to determine whether the agent misrepresented pricing or policy.

items.label: Conduct Monthly Retrieval Accuracy Audits
items.description: Sample a minimum of 200 agent responses per month and manually verify that retrieved content accurately reflects current product catalog, pricing, and policy data. Track accuracy rate by source type and set a minimum acceptable accuracy threshold of 99.5% for pricing and policy responses.

items.label: Maintain an Agent Change Log with Rollback Capability
items.description: Version-control all changes to the agent's query planning logic, retrieval configuration, synthesis prompts, and knowledge base indexes. Maintain the ability to roll back to any prior configuration within 30 minutes in response to a detected compliance or accuracy incident.

items.label: Train Customer Support Teams on Agent Failure Modes
items.description: Deliver quarterly training to customer support staff covering the agent's known failure modes (pricing hallucination, policy misrepresentation, stale inventory data), escalation procedures, and how to retrieve agent response logs to support dispute resolution.

items.label: Publish an Internal AI Governance Policy for the RAG Agent
items.description: Document and publish an internal governance policy that defines ownership, acceptable use boundaries, prohibited query types, escalation thresholds, and review cadences for the agentic RAG system. Assign a named accountable owner and review the policy semi-annually.

items.label: Track and Report Agent-Attributed Revenue Impact
items.description: Instrument the agent to track conversion events, refund rates, and dispute rates attributable to agent-assisted sessions. Report these metrics monthly to leadership to quantify both the value and the risk exposure of the agentic system.

notes.note: Under FTC policy, all objective product claims surfaced by the agent must be substantiated by competent and reliable evidence at the time the claim is made. Because the agent synthesizes claims dynamically from retrieved sources, each synthesis must be traceable to a specific, verifiable source document. Ungrounded superlatives, fabricated comparison claims, or AI-generated endorsements without disclosure may constitute deceptive advertising and trigger FTC enforcement action.

The agentic retrieval pipeline may process personal data across multiple sources (purchase history, browsing behavior, support tickets). Under CCPA and GDPR, customers have rights to access, deletion, and opt-out of sale/processing. The RAG index, vector store, and query logs must be included in DSR fulfillment workflows. Failure to propagate deletion requests to all retrieval layers within statutory timeframes (45 days under CCPA, 30 days under GDPR) constitutes a regulatory violation.

PCI DSS Requirement 1.3 mandates that systems outside the cardholder data environment (CDE) cannot directly access CDE systems. The RAG agent must be architecturally isolated from payment processing systems, and any query that could return cardholder data (card numbers, CVVs, bank account details) must be blocked at the network and application layer. Retrieval logs must not persist payment card data, and the agent's synthesis outputs must be scanned to ensure no cardholder data is included in responses.

Under the ADA and DOJ guidance on web accessibility, e-commerce platforms must ensure that AI-generated content is accessible to users with disabilities. Agent responses delivered via chat or embedded UI must meet WCAG 2.1 AA standards, including sufficient color contrast for inline citations and disclosures (minimum 4.5:1 ratio), keyboard-accessible response interfaces, and ARIA labels for dynamic content updates. Non-compliant AI interfaces expose the retailer to ADA Title III litigation.

cta.heading: Automate Your E-Commerce Agent Compliance
cta.description: Use Vex to continuously monitor your Agentic RAG pipeline for pricing hallucinations, policy misrepresentation, and retrieval provenance gaps—so you stay ahead of FTC enforcement and customer disputes before they cost you revenue.
